pass1_value.note: Pricing documents show credit-based plans and that a credit card is required for paid billing/overages, but the public docs do not explicitly state whether a developer can self-register and obtain free trial credits or immediate self-serve access for programmatic use without human approval.

pass1_value.note: Public developer-facing material describes a hosted MCP server (https://mcp.higgsfield.ai), connector manifests, and webhooks (programmatic endpoints). There is no separate, extensive REST or GraphQL reference site; the MCP page is the authoritative programmatic doc, so the surface appears to be a narrow REST-like MCP server interface rather than a broad documented public REST/GraphQL API.

Based on the disputed finding, the surface should be treated as a narrow MCP/HTTP-style interface rather than a broad public REST/GraphQL API; however confidence is low without retrievable primary text.

pass1_value.note: MCP developer docs and a CLI exist showing how to authenticate and call the MCP server programmatically. Building is possible today once you sign in (CLI or web) and have account credits. The main operational blocker is account provisioning/credits (pricing/billing) rather than missing technical docs.
